business.headline: PM Modi, Japan PM to inaugurate Maruti's Rs 35,000-crore mega car factory todayBuilt with a planned investment of around Rs 35,000 crore, the facility is expected to create more than 21,000 direct jobs while adding an annual production capacity of 10 lakh vehicles once fully operational.

Sensex jumps nearly 400 points, Nifty tops 24,100 as IT stocks reboundIT stocks led the rally, while falling crude oil prices and easing geopolitical concerns boosted investor confidence. However, a weak monsoon remains a key concern.

OTT subscriptions becoming a trap? 8 in 10 users say they're being manipulatedA nationwide survey has found that eight in ten OTT users in India say they have encountered 'dark patterns', deceptive design practices that nudge users into spending more, continuing subscriptions or making choices they may not have intended.

Energy crisis: Is the worst now over? Sajjid Chinoy respondsPrices of commercial LPG were cut by Rs 183.50 per 19-kg cylinder on Wednesday – the first reduction this year following a series of hikes in the wake of the West Asia crisis that caused energy supply disruptions across the globe.

Red Bull, PepsiCo, Sting among 6 energy drink brands get FSSAI noticesThe FSSAI issued notices to six energy drink brands -- Red Bull Energy Drink, PepsiCo's Adrenaline Rush Energy Drink, Reliance Consumer Products' Campa Energy Drink Gold Boost, Sting Energy Drink, Hell Energy and Coca-Cola-backed Monster Energy -- over misbranding and misleading claims.

PPF, NSC, SCSS interest rates for July-September 2026 declared: Has anything changed?The Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) and Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) will continue to offer 8.2%, the highest among the major small savings schemes.

Indian women are taking charge of their money. Here's how they are investingThe image of women as only careful savers is fast changing. Today, more Indian women are investing, exploring digital platforms and taking control of their financial future.

EPFO extends online service outage till July 2: What does it mean for members?EPAs part of the upgrade, members will not be able to submit fresh claims through the EPFO portal during the maintenance period. The organisation has also temporarily stopped the processing of claims while the migration work is underway.

Sensex rises over 440 points, Nifty closes above 24,000; IT stocks cap gainsFinancial, auto and FMCG stocks powered the rally, while weakness in IT shares and concerns over a weak monsoon kept investors cautious.

RBI's latest worry isn't inflation. It's artificial intelligenceArtificial intelligence is no longer just transforming banking—it is emerging as one of the biggest threats to the financial system. In its latest Financial Stability Report, the RBI has warned that AI-powered cyberattacks, dependence on common AI platforms and growing technology risks could pose fresh challenges for banks and financial institutions.

IndiGo's new budget-friendly 'Lite' fare for cabin baggage flyers takes off July 15According to the airline, the fare is designed for passengers travelling with only cabin baggage. By paying only for the services they use, customers can have greater control over their travel costs.

A rare NRI investment opportunity, lost in paperwork? Nithin Kamath thinks soThe Zerodha co-founder says lengthy onboarding processes may prevent NRIs from taking advantage of attractive FCNR deposit rates enabled by a recent RBI move.

8th Pay Commission: From Rs 18,000 to Rs 69,000? Minimum pay debate explainedEmployee organisations believe the current minimum basic pay of Rs 18,000, introduced nearly a decade ago under the 7th Pay Commission, no longer reflects the cost of living. Depending on the method used, unions have proposed increasing it to anywhere between Rs 55,000 and nearly Rs 69,000.

7 big financial changes from July 1 every Indian must knowJuly has brought several important financial changes that could affect your everyday life. From ITR filing deadlines and passport fees to Aadhaar updates, banking rules and credit card benefits, here's what has changed from July 1.

Why India's energy scare may be ending sooner than expectedFuel prices have started easing in India as crude oil retreats, Nayara cuts pump rates and commercial LPG gets cheaper. The shift points to improving supplies after the West Asia conflict, though volatility remains.

Market open higher as auto stocks lead; monsoon worries keep gains in checkIndian benchmark indices opened higher as auto stocks led a recovery from Tuesday's losses. Gains stayed limited as monsoon concerns and stalled US-Iran talks kept investors cautious.

Gold prices fall, but buyers disappear as demand drops 70%Gold demand in India has fallen by over 70%, as higher import duty, subdued buying sentiment and a sharp correction in prices keep consumers away. With MCX gold down over Rs 50,000 from its record high, here's why demand has slumped and what experts expect next.

Nayara Energy cuts petrol price by Rs 5, diesel by Rs 3 as global oil rates coolNayara Energy has cut petrol prices by Rs 5 per litre and diesel by Rs 3 per litre across its nationwide retail network, becoming the first fuel retailer to reduce prices in over two years. The move follows a decline in global crude oil prices after easing tensions in West Asia.

Insurance mis-selling: IRDAI wants every policy linked to person who sold itIRDAI has proposed tagging every insurance policy to the individual who sold it instead of only the bank or intermediary. The move aims to improve accountability in insurance mis-selling cases and strengthen consumer protection.

Can you claim your EPF pension before 58? Here's what every employee must knowEarly retirement is becoming increasingly common, but what happens to your EPF pension if you stop working before 58? Can you start receiving it right away? Let us find out.

entertainment.headline: Missed Obsession in theatres? Here's how you can watch it now Obsession has made its digital debut on premium video-on-demand platforms following its successful theatrical run. While viewers can currently rent or purchase the film, its release on a subscription-based streaming platform is still awaited.

Ramayana teaser VFX row: Content creator claims makers released poor cut for buzz A content creator who visited the Ramayana sets claimed the makers released an inferior Lord Rama teaser on purpose. The remark, later deleted and recirculated through screenshots, has revived debate over the film's VFX.

AI or real? Viral Ranbir Kapoor Ramayana video triggers VFX, authenticity debate Actor Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana is back in the spotlight after a viral video claiming to show a scene from the film surfaced online. The clip has sparked discussions around its VFX quality and whether the footage is real or AI-generated.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan eyes July release as censor clearance buzz intensifies Unconfirmed reports say Vijay's Jana Nayagan could reach theatres in July after a long delay. The expected clearance from the censor board this week may help the makers lock the release plan.

Taylor-Travis wedding buzz: Police confirm security, Mamdani jokes 'stay inside' New York officials are preparing security cover around Madison Square Garden amid reports of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding celebrations. Amid the speculation, Mayor Zohran Mamdani's joking remark has left everyone curious.

Welcome To The Jungle box office Day 6: Akshay's film crosses Rs 125 crore worldwide Welcome To The Jungle crossed the Rs 125 crore mark at the worldwide box office on Day 6 despite witnessing a slight dip in its India collections. Akshay Kumar's comedy has now earned Rs 129.20 crore globally and Rs 87.50 crore nett in India.

Supergirl in super mess: Superman legacy led to Milly Alcock's $100-million crash? Milly Alcock's Supergirl is likely to incur at least $100-million loss. The threat of ending up DC's biggest box office disaster ever also looms large. What went wrong with Supergirl?

Minions & Monsters review: A delightful ode to Hollywood, friendship, big dreamsRelease Date:Thu Jul 02, 2026 Minions & Monsters movie review: The new instalment follows James, Henry and Ed as chaos turns them into Hollywood stars. The film uses that rise and fall to tell a warm story about dreams, friendship and loyalty.

Randeep Hooda ditches car for metro ride after shoot; video goes viral Actor Randeep Hooda was seen travelling on the Mumbai Metro after wrapping up his shoot, opting to avoid Mumbai's rain-hit traffic. A video of his commute has since gone viral on social media.

Kangana Ranaut shreds Ram Kapoor for his attitude in Lock Upp: Don't defend Actor-politician Kangana Ranaut slammed Ram Kapoor in the Lock Upp 2 promo, questioning his attitude and why he joined the reality show. The heated exchange will air in the upcoming Judgement Day episode on July 4.

Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce's wedding: Venue staff to follow strict no-phone policy Fresh reports claim strict security measures are in place for singer Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce's rumoured wedding, with venue staff allegedly barred from carrying phones during work. Employees are also said to face additional security checks as speculation around the high-profile celebration continues.

Farah Khan 'thrilled and proud' after Academy invites her to Oscar voting panel Farah Khan has shared a major career update after receiving an invitation from the Academy. The latest announcement also features several prominent Indian film personalities. Here's who made the list and why it matters.

Ram Kapoor flaunts being 1st TV actor to perform lip kiss, says wife was proud Ram Kapoor has revealed how wife Gautami Kapoor reacted before his much-talked-about on-screen kiss with Sakshi Tanwar. His latest comments come amid the ongoing debate over his views on marriage and relationships.

Kala Hiran producer vows 8,000-screen release amid Salman Khan case Kala Hiran producer Amit Jani spoke about how they are planning to release the film amid the court case. The film has not yet been submitted to the Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC) for certification.

The Odyssey final trailer: Matt Damon leads epic Trojan Horse battle Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey released its final trailer on 1 July 2026. The footage underlines the scale of the IMAX retelling ahead of the film's 17 July release.

Ram Kapoor slammed for kissing co-contestant Varun 'Laila' Yadav in Lock Upp 2 A Lock Upp 2 episode showed actor Ram Kapoor grabbing Varun 'Laila' Yadav's face and kissing him during a conversation in the garden area. The moment drew sharp criticism online, with viewers raising questions of consent and conduct.

Raakh actor Ramandeep Yadav: I wanted Rajjo's eyes to make people uncomfortable Actor Ramandeep Yadav opened up about his year-long preparation for Raakh, studying criminal psychology, avoiding stereotypes, and overcoming typecasting.

Tragedy of Hera Pheri 3: Priyadarshan just confirmed what the fans feared most Director Priyadarshan has confirmed he is no longer associated with Hera Pheri 3 amid legal issues and personal conflicts. His exit has deepened fears that the long-delayed film may never reach screens, leaving a cherished franchise in limbo.

Gautami Kapoor reacts after Ram Kapoor's Lock Upp infidelity comment goes viral Actor Gautami Kapoor has reacted after her husband Ram Kapoor's controversial Lock Upp comments on infidelity and marriage went viral. Her latest Instagram posts have left fans talking.

general news.headline: WhatsApp is bringing usernames, what does it mean for privacy and cybersecurity?WhatsApp has announced that it is bringing usernames to the app. But this has sparked debate over how useful and secure usernames could be compared to phone numbers. And will usernames actually be used by people in India?

Toddlers put in washing machine, abused with jet spray in Bengaluru daycare horrorDisturbing visuals from a daycare operating inside Bengaluru's Capgemini campus have sparked widespread outrage, prompting police to register an FIR against five women caregivers and launch an investigation into the alleged abuse of children.

How Vijay govt is saving big for Tamil Nadu, and contractors are thanking himTamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay-led government's decision to open even the smallest public works projects for civic tender has resulted in contractors quoting sharply lower rates, resulting in big gains for the state's exchequer.

Why can't India build a decent visa website, asks The Economist. Indians respondThe Economist's criticism of India's visa and other government websites has sparked strong reactions on social media. While some slammed the London-based magazine for its alleged biased views, others agreed with its opinion, and said the user experience needed to be improved.

Chetan Bhagat's viral take on Siya case, Indian parenting and regressive societyIn an exclusive interview with India Today, author and columnist Chetan Bhagat addressed the intense public scrutiny surrounding his column on the Pune murder mystery involving Siya Goyal.

PM Modi, Japan PM to inaugurate Maruti's Rs 35,000-crore mega car factory todayBuilt with a planned investment of around Rs 35,000 crore, the facility is expected to create more than 21,000 direct jobs while adding an annual production capacity of 10 lakh vehicles once fully operational.

Why Mumbai's roads are full of water, but taps at homes are dryOne of the season's first intense spells of monsoon rain has inundated Mumbai's roads and subways. But, the reservoirs and lakes supplying drinking water to Mumbai are at seven percent of their capacity. We explain what's behind this paradox.

This mini electric SUV is set to hit Indian roads this yearThe VinFast VF 3, set to arrive in India later this year, aims to bring affordable urban electric mobility with compact dimensions, SUV-inspired styling and a claimed driving range of up to 210 km.

Watch: Russian couple climbs to top of NYC Empire State Building, then get engagedDressed in sleeveless black outfits and wearing face coverings, Angela Nikolau and Vanya Beerkus climbed to the top of the 1,454-foot skyscraper and stood on the narrow antenna while documenting the feat on social media.

Live: Guard of Honour for Japan PM at Rashtrapati BhavanJapan PM India Visit Live Updates: In her first ever trip to India, Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi arrived in New Delhi for a three-day official visit. During her visit, Takaichi will hold wide-ranging talks with Prime Minister Narendra Modi at the 16th India-Japan Annual Summit, with discussions expected to focus on trade, investment, defence, technology and strengthening the Special Strategic and Global Partnership between the two nations. According to the Ministry of External Affairs, Takaichi will receive a ceremonial welcome at Rashtrapati Bhavan at 10 am today before meeting PM Modi at Hyderabad House. The two leaders will hold bilateral talks, followed by the exchange of MoUs and press statements at aroud 1 pm. Takaichi will address the India-Japan Business Forum at Hotel Taj Palace at 3 pm. The Japanese leader is scheduled to depart for Tokyo on Friday morning. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

A Chinese corridor to India's east? China plans Bangladesh route after Pak CPECAfter pouring billions of dollars into the CPEC in Pakistan, Beijing is now planning a corridor on India's eastern flank through Bangladesh and Myanmar. During Bangladeshi PM Tarique Rahman's China visit, Beijing proposed the construction of a multi-modal trade route to Bangladesh. What does it mean for India?

First he cheats, then calls: Old video shows Siya abusing on phone while partyingAn old video has surfaced showing Siya Goyal drinking at a pub and using abusive language during a heated phone call, in which she is heard saying, "First he cheats on me, then he calls me."

Kangana Ranaut shreds Ram Kapoor for his attitude in Lock Upp: Don't defendActor-politician Kangana Ranaut slammed Ram Kapoor in the Lock Upp 2 promo, questioning his attitude and why he joined the reality show. The heated exchange will air in the upcoming Judgement Day episode on July 4.

8th Pay Commission: From Rs 18,000 to Rs 69,000? Minimum pay debate explainedEmployee organisations believe the current minimum basic pay of Rs 18,000, introduced nearly a decade ago under the 7th Pay Commission, no longer reflects the cost of living. Depending on the method used, unions have proposed increasing it to anywhere between Rs 55,000 and nearly Rs 69,000.

Harry Kane's rocket sinks DR Congo, breaks Pele's World Cup record in styleHarry Kane scored twice as England came from behind to beat DR Congo 2-1 and reach the Round of 16. His late winner took him past Pele's World Cup tally as DR Congo coach Sebastien Desabre hailed him as the world's best striker.

Sena defections engineered by Eknath Shinde to weaken Fadnavis: Aaditya ThackerayWhen asked whether the Shiv Sena (UBT) suffers from a perception problem of being family-run organisations, Aaditya Thackeray said he was "very proud" of his family legacy and insisted that political legitimacy ultimately comes from electoral acceptance.

Explained: Why has India asked WhatsApp to pause username rollout?India has ordered Meta to pause the WhatsApp username feature amid concerns of privacy and security of users. But why? Here is everything you need to know about the new feature, and what it means for you.

Assam man provides 15 documents, but fails to prove he's an Indian. Here's whyA man in Assam submitted 15 documents, including 1951 NRC records containing his family's names, but was declared a foreigner. The Gauhati High Court has now upheld the decision by a Foreigners' Tribunal. This is why the man failed to prove his Indian citizenship.

Supergirl in super mess: Superman legacy led to Milly Alcock's $100-million crash?Milly Alcock's Supergirl is likely to incur at least $100-million loss. The threat of ending up DC's biggest box office disaster ever also looms large. What went wrong with Supergirl?

Chinese firm launches companion robots with silicon skin and Emotional AIImagine having a robot that remembers your conversations, reads your emotions and even maintains eye contact. That's exactly what Chinese robotics company UBTech is promising with its new humanoid companion, as it looks to bring AI-powered robots from factories into everyday homes.

Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce's wedding: Venue staff to follow strict no-phone policyFresh reports claim strict security measures are in place for singer Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce's rumoured wedding, with venue staff allegedly barred from carrying phones during work. Employees are also said to face additional security checks as speculation around the high-profile celebration continues.

Rain lashes several parts of Delhi-NCR; red alert issued, thunderstorms predictedThe IMD upgraded its weather warning from a yellow alert to a red alert as rain along with intense thunderstorms and squally winds swept across the region, bringing much-needed relief from the hot and humid conditions that had persisted over the past few days.

Russia taps India for petrol to ease fuel shortages after Ukraine's drone strikesRussia has started importing petrol from India as fuel shortages spread across its 11 time zones, with rationing, long queues at filling stations and record petrol price increases reported in several regions.

Ram Mandir donation theft: Cops probe bribe-for-job charge in recruitment driveAyodhya Police have widened the Ram Temple donation case to examine alleged bribes for appointments at the temple establishment. The probe now spans recruitment records, bank trails, trust-linked assets and the role of a trust member under scrutiny.

sports.headline: Djokovic defends Serena Williams's Wimbledon return: Just enjoy her greatness Novak Djokovic has urged fans to celebrate Serena Williams's return to Wimbledon rather than rush to criticise her after the 44-year-old's comeback ended in a first-round defeat.

England out to break South Africa curse with Nat Sciver-Brunt boost for semis Women's T20 World Cup, ENG vs SA: England have lost their last two knockout matches against South Africa and will be eager to snap that streak when the two sides meet in the second semi-final at The Kennington Oval in London on Thursday.

Sooryavanshi's wait for India debut will put him under more pressure: Gavaskar Batting great Sunil Gavaskar believes delaying Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's senior India debut will only increase the pressure on the teenage opener, who was once again left out of the playing XI for the opening T20I against England on Wednesday.

10-man USA keep dream alive, reach last 16 with win over Bosnia & Herzegovina Malik Tillman spared the United States an anxious finish with a sublime late free-kick as the co-hosts defeated Bosnia and Herzegovina 2-0 on Wednesday to reach the World Cup Round of 16, despite playing the final stages with 10 men after Folarin Balogun's dismissal.

Heartbreaking moment as DR Congo coach learns of father's death after World Cup exit Moments after DR Congo's 1-2 defeat to England ended their historic World Cup run, coach Sebastien Desabre was informed of his father's death during the post-match press conference.

Thomas Tuchel urges parents to let children stay up for England's clash with Mexico England manager Thomas Tuchel may have slept through Mexico's victory over Ecuador, but after his side's dramatic 2-1 comeback win over DR Congo sealed a place in the World Cup Round of 16, he had a message for parents back home: let the children stay up to watch Sunday's showdown at the Azteca Stadium.

USA 2-0 BIH Live: Balogun, Tillman fire USA into Round of 16 USA vs Bosnia & Herzegovina (BIH) Live, FIFA World Cup: Full-time: The United States are into the Round of 16 after a composed 2-0 victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina in San Francisco. Folarin Balogun broke the deadlock late in the first half before Malik Tillman doubled the advantage after the interval to seal progression. The hosts also overcame Balogun's second-half red card, producing a disciplined defensive display to see out the contest with 10 men and book their place in the next round.

Senegal's night of promise ends in heartbreak as Belgium pull off the great escape Belgium completed a stunning comeback from 2-0 down to beat Senegal 3-2 after extra time, with Youri Tielemans converting a controversial late VAR-awarded penalty. The Lions of Teranga were left heartbroken after seeing a famous World Cup upset slip away.

Harry Kane on Lionel Messi's level? Gordon makes big GOAT comparison after England win FIFA World Cup 2026: Anthony Gordon hailed Harry Kane after the England captain's brace turned a fraught tie against DR Congo. The comeback win sent England into the last 16 against co-hosts Mexico and underlined Kane's authority.

Novak Djokovic pranks ball girl, then outclasses Stefanos Tsitsipas to reach Round 3 Novak Djokovic entertained Centre Court with a playful prank on a ball girl before cruising past Stefanos Tsitsipas 6-3, 6-4, 6-2 to reach the third round of Wimbledon 2026 on Wednesday.

Harry Kane's rocket sinks DR Congo, breaks Pele's World Cup record in style Harry Kane scored twice as England came from behind to beat DR Congo 2-1 and reach the Round of 16. His late winner took him past Pele's World Cup tally as DR Congo coach Sebastien Desabre hailed him as the world's best striker.

Shreyas Iyer's wait for first win as India captain prolongs after Durham T20I washoutENG vs IND, 1st T20I: Abhishek Sharma's blistering 59 and Shreyas Iyer's fluent 68 powered India to 189 for 7 in the opening T20I against England, but persistent rain prevented the hosts from beginning their chase in Durham.

Kohli training with Sanjay Bangar in viral video fuels England ODI return buzzVirat Kohli's training session with former India batting coach Sanjay Bangar has set social media abuzz ahead of the England ODI series. With his return nearing after retiring from Tests and T20Is, fans are hoping the hard work in the nets translates into another vintage Kohli chapter.

South Africa not at peak, but England will be under pressure: Laura WolvaardtWomen's T20 World Cup, ENG vs SA: Speaking to reporters ahead of the semi-final at The Oval, Laura Wolvaardt admitted that South Africa are yet to produce their best cricket in the tournament, but said her side would look to capitalise on the pressure England will be under in the knockout clash.

French Open champion Mirra Andreeva makes shock exit in Round 2Barbora Krejcikova battled back from a set down to defeat Mirra Andreeva 4-6, 7-5, 6-4, knocking the reigning French Open champion out in the second round of Wimbledon.

BEL vs SEN Highlights: Late controversial penalty sends Belgium past Senegal 3-2Belgium vs Senegal Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (BEL 3-2 SEN) Belgium staged a dramatic comeback to beat Senegal in a tense knockout clash, overturning a late deficit before edging a controversial extra-time finish. Senegal will feel hard done by after dominating large spells but falling short in the decisive moments.

Nat Sciver-Brunt plays down title talk as England seek to end World Cup droughtWomen's T20 World Cup, ENG vs SA: Speaking to the reporters before the semis against South Africa, Nat Sciver-Brunt said that England aren't worried about not winning world titles since 2017 and will go one match at a time to reach their goal.

Captain Kane to the rescue as England survive DR Congo scareFIFA World Cup 2026: Harry Kane rescued England from a major scare with a decisive brace, overturning a shock DR Congo lead in a tense World Cup knockout clash. Despite a disciplined and fearless performance from the Leopards, England's quality and late momentum shift secured their progression.

Abhishek Sharma scripts history, becomes fastest batter to 100 T20I sixesAbhishek Sharma scripted history by becoming the fastest batter to 100 T20I sixes, reaching the milestone in just 785 balls before smashing a blistering 59 off 24 against England in the first T20I.

Jannik Sinner fights past Nuno Borges, Aryna Sabalenka survives McCartney Kessler scareDefending champion Jannik Sinner and world No.1 Aryna Sabalenka advanced to the Wimbledon third round after overcoming stern second-round tests on Wednesday. Sinner beat Nuno Borges in straight sets, while Sabalenka saved four set points before beating McCartney Kessler.

No Vaibhav Sooryavanshi debut in Durham as India ignore Gavaskar's suggestionThe wait for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut continues after the 15-year-old was left out of the first T20I against England despite growing calls for his inclusion. India stuck with their World Cup-winning core, ignoring Sunil Gavaskar's advice to hand the teenager an immediate debut.

Naomi Osaka conquers Wimbledon nerves, keeps daughter's birthday plans intact4-time Grand Slam champion Naomi Osaka eased past Anastasia Gasanova to reach the Wimbledon third round before revealing her daughter's birthday had made her unusually nervous.

ENG 2 -1 DRC Highlights: Kane double seals comeback win for EnglandEngland (ENG) vs DR Congo (DRC) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (ENG 2 - 1 DRC) Harry Kane's second-half brace inspired England to a hard-fought 2-1 comeback win over DR Congo after the Three Lions had spent much of the afternoon chasing the game. Anthony Gordon changed the contest off the bench with two assists as England booked a Round of 16 meeting with Mexico.

Sooryavanshi should write exams, play gully cricket: Ex-cricketer fires a warningFormer South Africa cricketer Daryll Cullinan has urged cricket to slow down on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi despite the clamour for his India debut. He argues the teenager needs space, exams and guidance before the IPL and online scrutiny harden brilliance into burden.

Injury blow for Serena Williams, doubles with sister Venus in doubtWimbledon 2026: Serena Williams suffered a knee injury scare after her first-round loss to Maya Joint in singles. The setback has cast doubt over her scheduled doubles return with Venus Williams later this week.

Day 3 Highlights: Novak Djokovic hammers Stefanos Tsitsipas, Mirra Andreeva knocked outWimbledon 2026 Highlights Day 3: French Open champion Mirra Andreeva has been knocked out after losing to Barbora Krejcikova in the second round. Novak Djokovic is in action against Stefanos Tsitsipas.

Uruguay did not want to learn: Bielsa bids goodbye with 100-min press conferenceMarcelo Bielsa signed off as Uruguay head coach with a brutally honest assessment of his tenure, saying his ideas never resonated with the squad as he reflected on the team's group-stage exit from the FIFA World Cup.

T20I captaincy was no surprise, it is not outlandish: Shreyas IyerSpeaking to the BCCI on the eve of India's T20I series in England, Shreyas Iyer said he was not surprised when the job came to him. Iyer replaced T20 World Cup-winning captain Suryakumar Yadav.

The Kylian Mbappe magic that made France's bereaved coach Deschamps smileKylian Mbappe's first instinct after scoring for France was not to celebrate with the crowd, but to run to Didier Deschamps. The emotional moment came just four days after the France coach returned to the World Cup camp following his mother's funeral.

The Vikings are coming: Haaland stands in the way of Brazil in FIFA World CupErling Haaland's winning goal sent Norway into the Round of 16 of the FIFA World Cup 2026. The team will now face 5-time champions Brazil in their second knockout fixture.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
